memberdef.name: ADDRESS_1_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* MSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_2_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Middle byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_3_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 3 /* LSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: DUMMY_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Dummy byte offset for fast, dual and quad reads */

memberdef.name: DUMMY_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* Number of dummy bytes for fast, dual and quad reads */

memberdef.name: RD_ID_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Read ID command + 3 bytes ID response */

memberdef.name: BANK_SEL_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* BRWR or EARWR command + 1 byte bank value */

memberdef.name: RD_CFG_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* 1 byte Configuration register + RD CFG command*/

memberdef.name: WR_CFG_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 3 /* WRR command + 1 byte each Status and Config Reg*/

memberdef.name: LQSPI_CR_1_DUMMY_BYTE
memberdef.initializer: 0x00000100 /* 1 Dummy Byte between address and return data */

briefdescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XQspiPs device driver in single, parallel and stacked modes using flash devices greater than 128Mb.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads and writes data in I/O mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPIPS driver to use.
contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the sectors in the serial Flash connected to the QSPI interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

memberdef.argsstring: (XQspiPs *QspiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command, u8 *WriteBfrPtr)
memberdef.name: FlashWrite

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial Flash connected to the QSPI interface.

detaileddescription.para: All the data put into the buffer must be in the same page of the device with page boundaries being on 256 byte boundaries.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address to write data to in the Flash.
contains the number of bytes to write.
is the command used to write data to the flash. QSPI device supports only Page Program command to write data to the flash.
is the pointer to the write buffer(which is to be transmitted)

briefdescription.para: This function reads serial Flash ID connected to the SPI interface.

detaileddescription.para: It then deduces the make and size of the flash and obtains the connection mode to point to corresponding parameters in the flash configuration table. The flash driver will function based on this and it presently supports Micron and Spansion - 128, 256 and 512Mbit and Winbond 128Mbit

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
is pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)
is pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if read id, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

memberdef.argsstring: (XQspiPs *QspiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command, u8 *WriteBfrPtr, u8 *ReadBfrPtr)
memberdef.name: FlashRead

briefdescription.para: This function performs an I/O read.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.
is the command used to read data from the flash. Supports normal, fast, dual and quad read commands.
is pointer to the write buffer which contains data to be transmitted
is pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written

briefdescription.para: This functions selects the current bank.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
to the write buffer which contains data to be transmitted.
is the bank to be selected in the flash device(s).

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if bank selected, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: This functions performs a bulk erase operation when the flash device has a single die.

detaileddescription.para: Works for both Spansion and Micron

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
is the pointer to command+address to be sent

briefdescription.para: This functions performs a die erase operation on all the die in the flash device.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses the die erase command for Micron 512Mbit and 1Gbit

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
is the pointer to command+address to be sent

briefdescription.para: This functions translates the address based on the type of interconnection.

detaileddescription.para: In case of stacked, this function asserts the corresponding slave select.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
which is to be accessed (for erase, write or read)

simplesect.para: RealAddr is the translated address - for single it is unchanged; for stacked, the lower flash size is subtracted; for parallel the address is divided by 2.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the QSPI Flash example.
This file contains a design example using the QSPI driver (XQspiPs) with a serial Flash device greater than 128Mb.

detaileddescription.para: The example writes to flash and reads it back in I/O mode. This examples performs some transfers in Auto mode and Manual start mode, to illustrate the modes available. It is recommended to use Manual CS + Auto start for best performance. This example illustrates single, parallel and stacked modes. Both the flash devices have to be of the same make and size. The hardware which this example runs on, must have a serial Flash (Micron N25Q or Spansion S25FL) for it to run. This example has been tested with the Micron Serial Flash (N25Q256, N25Q512 & N25Q00AA) and Spansion (S25FL256 & S25FL512)

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
2.02a hk 05/07/13 First release raw 12/10/15 Added support for Macronix 256Mb and 1Gb flash parts ms 04/05/17 Modified Comment lines in functions to recognize it as documentation block for doxygen generation. tjs 06/16/17 Added support for IS25LP256D flash part (PR-4650) 3.10 akm 08/17/22 Fix logical error in NumSect calculation. 3.11 akm 07/10/23 Add support for system device-tree flow for example. 3.14 sb 03/25/25 Fixed gcc and g++ warnings. 3.15 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
